meta_title: Roofing in South Broward, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers expert roof replacements & repairs in South Broward, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Call today!
h1: South Broward's Trusted Roofing Specialists — Built to Outlast Every Storm
intro:
South Broward is a community of hardworking homeowners who take pride in their properties — and with average home values around $290,000, protecting that investment starts from the top down. Your roof is the single most critical barrier between your family and South Florida's relentless heat, heavy rain, and hurricane-force winds. Yet in a ZIP code like 33023, where more than half of all homes were built before 1994, far too many roofs are quietly aging past their safe service life, leaving homeowners exposed to sudden leaks, structural damage, and skyrocketing insurance premiums. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we help South Broward residents get ahead of those risks with full roof replacements and targeted repairs designed for the realities of this specific climate and building stock.

Tucked between Hollywood to the east, West Park to the north, and Pembroke Park to the west, South Broward is a close-knit residential community where word-of-mouth still matters and neighbors talk. Families here have weathered some of Florida's most punishing storms, from the catastrophic winds of Hurricane Andrew to the widespread tile and shingle damage inflicted by Hurricane Wilma and Hurricane Irma. Properties near South Broward Park and throughout the surrounding residential streets show the long-term toll that repeated tropical systems take on roofing materials — especially older three-tab shingles and deteriorating underlayment that were never designed to handle modern wind-load requirements. A roof that passed inspection twenty years ago may not meet today's Florida Building Code standards or satisfy your current insurance carrier's requirements.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every South Broward roofing project. Our team is 100% in-house — no subcontractors, no middlemen — meaning the same skilled, licensed professionals who give you a quote are the ones on your roof on installation day. We install industry-leading products from CertainTeed and West Lake, both rated to handle South Florida's demanding wind and weather conditions. Whether you're managing a modest budget or planning a full tile transformation, we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making a durable, code-compliant new roof accessible for every South Broward homeowner.

section_why.h2: Why South Broward Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roof Replacements
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Broward isn't a decision homeowners should make lightly. This is a competitive market full of storm chasers, unlicensed operators, and out-of-state crews who flood South Florida after every major hurricane and disappear just as quickly. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation the right way — one completed roof at a time, with over 500 homes successfully served across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Our A+ Better Business Bureau rating and long list of verified local reviews reflect a company that shows up, does the work right, and stands behind it.

What truly sets us apart is our 100% in-house installation model. Every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roofing crew is a direct employee of the company — fully trained, background-checked, and covered under our licensing and insurance. We never hand your project off to a subcontractor. This matters enormously in South Broward, where pre-1994 home construction often reveals unexpected decking issues, rotted fascia, or outdated flashing details that require real-time problem-solving on the roof. Our experienced crews are empowered to address those discoveries correctly and immediately, without waiting for a subcontractor to reschedule or a project manager to relay information through layers of communication.

Our local knowledge is another critical advantage. We understand the specific permit requirements of the Broward County Building Division, the wind zone classifications that apply to the 33023 area, and the insurance documentation — including wind mitigation reports — that South Broward homeowners need to secure premium discounts from carriers like Citizens, Universal, and Heritage. We don't just install roofs; we guide our clients through the entire process from initial inspection to final permit sign-off, making a potentially stressful renovation feel manageable and straightforward.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also offers flexible financing options that align with South Broward's lower-middle income demographic. We believe every homeowner deserves a safe, hurricane-rated roof regardless of their savings situation. With $0 down and payments starting as low as $87 per month, we've helped hundreds of South Florida families protect their homes without financial strain. When you partner with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying a roof — you're investing in decades of protection backed by The Kings of Service.

section_history.h2: A Storm-Tested Community: South Broward's History with Hurricanes and Roof Damage
section_history.body:
South Broward has faced at least nine named storms since 1990, and the roofing damage accumulated across those events tells a sobering story. The first and most defining blow came in August 1992, when Hurricane Andrew made landfall just to the south and sent catastrophic winds surging through Broward County. While the most severe destruction was concentrated in Miami-Dade, Andrew's outer bands were strong enough to strip shingles, crack tile, and expose decking across thousands of South Broward homes. That event became the benchmark that rewrote Florida's building codes — but homes built before Andrew's landfall, which account for more than 55% of South Broward's housing stock, were constructed under the older, less demanding standards.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 became the storm that exposed how many pre-Andrew roofs had survived on borrowed time. Wilma's near-category-3 winds moved fast and wide across all of Broward County, generating widespread tile displacement, shingle blow-off, and underlayment failures that led to interior water damage in homes across the region. Neighborhoods throughout South Broward saw roofs that looked intact from the street but were saturated and structurally compromised underneath. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017 — a slower-moving storm that tested roofs already weakened by twelve years of post-Wilma repairs. Irma's sustained winds and prolific rainfall put the final nail in the coffin for many aging roof systems throughout the area.

For South Broward homeowners still carrying original or early-replacement roofs, the historical pattern is clear: every storm season carries real financial risk. A roof failure isn't just a repair bill — it's potential interior damage, mold remediation, displaced living, and an insurance claim that can push premiums higher or trigger non-renewal. The smartest and most cost-effective move is a proactive, code-compliant roof replacement before the next storm puts your home in its path.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance for South Broward Roof Replacements
section_code.body:
South Broward falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, and while the area is not classified as High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — it still requires fully hurricane-rated roofing products and installation methods. The distinction matters: non-HVHZ areas like the 33023 ZIP code are governed by standard FBC Chapter 15 roofing requirements and the ASCE 7 wind speed maps, which designate South Broward for a 170 mph design wind speed. That means every shingle, tile, fastener, and underlayment product installed on your home must be tested and approved to perform under those conditions.

All roofing permits in South Broward are issued and inspected through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the permit application process on behalf of every client — from submitting the required product approval documentation and engineering drawings to scheduling the county inspection and obtaining the final Certificate of Completion. Pulling permits is not optional; an unpermitted roof replacement creates serious liability for homeowners, can void manufacturer warranties, and creates complications when selling the property or filing an insurance claim.

For homes in the 33023 area built before 1994, code compliance often requires more than just new shingles. Older homes may need upgraded roof-to-wall connections, re-nailing of existing roof decking to meet current fastener schedules (typically 8d ring-shank nails at 6-inch spacing), or full deck replacement to meet structural load requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's inspection process identifies all of these factors upfront, so there are no surprises mid-project. We've completed hundreds of code-compliant roof replacements across Broward County and know exactly what the inspectors are looking for.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake Roofing Systems Installed in South Broward
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected roofing product lines available in South Florida: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product families are rigorously tested for hurricane-force wind conditions, carry Florida Product Approvals, and are backed by industry-leading warranties — making them the ideal choice for South Broward homeowners who demand both performance and longevity.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles are among the most popular roofing choices for South Broward homes, offering a dramatic upgrade from the outdated three-tab shingles found on many pre-1994 properties in the area. Landmark shingles feature a dual-layer construction that delivers superior wind resistance, tested to withstand wind speeds up to 130 mph when properly installed, with enhanced adhesive strips that activate under South Florida heat to seal shingles tightly against uplift. The Landmark Series also carries a Class A fire rating and comes in a wide range of colors designed to complement the exterior palettes common in South Broward neighborhoods. With CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty program, homeowners receive full coverage on both materials and workmanship — providing true peace of mind through every storm season.

For homeowners seeking the prestige, durability, and classic South Florida aesthetic of tile roofing, West Lake offers both concrete and clay tile systems that stand up to decades of UV exposure, heat cycling, and hurricane-force rain. Concrete tile from West Lake is engineered to meet or exceed Broward County's wind uplift requirements and can last 40 to 50 years with proper maintenance. Clay tile delivers an even longer lifespan with a naturally beautiful finish that resists fading. West Lake's Florida-approved tile profiles are available in a variety of styles — from flat to S-profile — giving South Broward homeowners significant design flexibility while meeting every applicable building code requirement.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in South Broward — and How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
For South Broward homeowners, understanding the true cost of a roof replacement requires looking at both the upfront investment and the long-term financial picture. In the current South Florida market, a full architectural shingle replacement on a typical single-family home in the 33023 area generally ranges from $12,000 to $18,000, depending on roof size, pitch, decking condition, and any necessary structural upgrades. Tile roof replacements typically range from $18,000 to $30,000 or more, reflecting the higher material and labor intensity involved. On a home valued at approximately $290,000, these investments represent 4% to 10% of total home value — a significant but highly justified expense given the structural and insurance benefits returned.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing understands that South Broward is a lower-middle income community where a major home improvement expense requires careful financial planning. That's exactly why we offer flexible financing options designed to put a hurricane-rated roof within reach for every homeowner. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month with $0 down, subject to approval, allowing you to begin protecting your home immediately without depleting your savings. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that match your budget, and our team walks you through every option during the consultation process — no pressure, no confusion.

Beyond the sticker price, the return on investment for a new roof in South Broward is compelling. A code-compliant roof replacement typically adds $10,000 to $20,000 in appraised home value, strengthens your position in a competitive real estate market, and — critically — can reduce your homeowners insurance premium by 15% to 40% annually through wind mitigation credits. For a homeowner currently paying $4,000 per year in insurance, that's up to $1,600 per year in savings, which meaningfully accelerates the payback period on the roofing investment.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof Can Dramatically Lower Your South Broward Homeowners Insurance
section_insurance.body:
Homeowners insurance in South Broward has become one of the most pressing financial concerns for residents in recent years. With carriers like Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance regularly adjusting rates upward in response to South Florida's storm exposure and claims history, many 33023 homeowners are paying premiums that have doubled or even tripled over the past decade. A new, code-compliant roof is one of the most powerful tools available to counteract those increases — and the savings can be substantial.

The mechanism behind insurance savings is the wind mitigation inspection, a standardized assessment conducted by a licensed inspector that evaluates your roof's ability to resist hurricane-force winds. Key factors include roof shape (hip roofs score best), roof-to-wall attachment method, roof deck attachment (nail size and spacing), and the presence of a secondary water resistance barrier — a self-adhering underlayment layer required on all new Broward County roofs. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing replaces your roof, every one of these factors is addressed to maximize your wind mitigation score. We also complete a post-installation inspection report to submit directly to your insurance carrier, expediting the discount process.

A superior wind mitigation score can reduce the wind portion of your homeowners insurance premium by anywhere from 15% to 45%, depending on your carrier and specific roof characteristics. For the average South Broward homeowner paying $3,500 to $5,000 annually in premiums, that translates to real, recurring savings every year for the life of the roof. Some homeowners recoup a significant portion of their roofing investment within five to seven years through insurance savings alone — before factoring in the avoided cost of storm-related repairs or the added home equity a new roof creates.

faqs.q: How do I know if my South Broward home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: A professional roof inspection is the best way to determine the right course of action. In South Broward, where over 55% of homes were built before 1994, many roofs are at or beyond the end of their designed service life. Signs that a full replacement is warranted include granule loss in gutters, cracked or curling shingles, multiple leak points, visible sagging, or a roof older than 20-25 years. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections to assess your specific situation and provide an honest recommendation — repair when practical, replace when necessary.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in South Broward, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in South Broward range from $12,000 to $30,000 depending on size, material choice, and existing conditions. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, subject to approval. We work with multiple lenders to find terms that fit your budget, making it possible to protect your home without waiting until you've saved the full project cost.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in South Broward, and who handles it?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for any full roof replacement in South Broward under Broward County Building Division regulations. An unpermitted roof can create legal, warranty, and insurance complications. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process for you — from submitting Florida Product Approval documentation to scheduling the county inspection and obtaining the final Certificate of Completion. You don't have to navigate the permit office yourself.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in South Broward?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in South Broward take one to three days for shingle systems and three to five days for tile, depending on roof size, complexity, and decking condition. Our 100% in-house crews work efficiently and maintain a clean worksite throughout. We coordinate permit scheduling in advance so inspections don't cause unnecessary delays. Before we begin, we'll give you a clear project timeline and keep you informed at every stage of the process.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in South Broward?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new, code-compliant roof with a proper wind mitigation inspection can reduce the wind portion of your homeowners insurance premium by 15% to 45% depending on your carrier and roof characteristics. South Broward homeowners who upgrade to hip roof configurations, proper deck attachment, and secondary water resistance barriers typically see the largest discounts. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs every roof to maximize your wind mitigation score and helps coordinate post-installation inspections to ensure your insurer receives the documentation needed to apply your credits.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your South Broward Home with a Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Get a free, no-pressure roof inspection from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — and discover how affordable a durable, code-compliant new roof can be with financing starting at just $87/month and $0 down.
